title: Shrimp Tacos with Cilantro-Lime Slaw
description: Seasoned shrimp seared until pink and lightly browned, served in warm corn tortillas with a creamy cilantro-lime slaw. Fast weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: frozen shrimp, peeled and deveined (tail off preferred)

instructions.text: Thaw shrimp: place frozen shrimp in a colander under cold running water for 5–10 minutes. Pat thoroughly dry with paper towels — this is critical for a good sear.
Season shrimp: toss with olive oil, taco seasoning, and lime juice. Set aside while you make the slaw.
Make the slaw: whisk together mayo or Greek yogurt, lime juice, honey, and a pinch of salt. Add coleslaw mix and cilantro and toss well. Taste and adjust salt or lime. Let sit to absorb flavor.
Cook the shrimp: heat a large skillet over medium-high. Add shrimp in a single layer and cook about 2 minutes per side until pink and lightly browned. Remove from heat.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet 30 seconds per side, or microwave wrapped in a damp towel for 30 seconds.
Assemble: layer slaw on the bottom of each tortilla, add 3–4 shrimp, then finish with optional toppings — avocado, hot sauce, cotija, pickled onions, lime wedge.

notes:
Quick drizzle sauce: mix 2 tbsp mayo or yogurt with 1 tbsp lime juice and a dash of hot sauce.

For extra crunch: add toasted pepitas or chopped radishes.

Tropical swap: replace slaw with mango salsa for a different flavor profile.

Greek yogurt in the slaw keeps it lighter than mayo and adds a slight tang that works well with the lime.
